description: The bird was on the ground but unseen as I left the creek bank and pushed through the brush.   I flushed it and it flew about 15ft and landed in a small bush about 12 inches up from the ground.   It was facing me when I was able to view it with my bins.   It had a totally gray head and throat and a complete white, circular eye-ring.   The lower body was dull yellow.   I lifted my camera and got a two photo burst of its profile before it dropped to the ground and out of sight.   I pished to bring it back without success.   I examined my photo and realized it might be a Conneticutt, so I used Merlin App on my phone to play the song.   No response.   I stayed in the immediate area about an additional 20min to try and re find the bird without success.
